steps.title: Convert the percent decrease
steps.content:
A $2\%$ decrease means each year keeps $98\%$ of the previous year's enrollment:
$$1-0.02=0.98$$

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
The initial enrollment in 2015 is $3{,}000$, and $x$ counts years after 2015. Use initial value times yearly multiplier:
$$f(x)=3{,}000(0.98)^x$$

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Choice D is the only function with initial value $3{,}000$ and decay factor $0.98$. Therefore, D is correct.
